model_narrative: We plan motor production across the four quarters of a year. In each quarter we decide how many motors to produce, which shifts to operate and how many workers to staff on each, how many workers to hire or fire, and how much inventory to carry into the next quarter. We also decide once for the year whether to lease an extra storage facility. Production on each shift follows a piecewise relationship between motors made and workers needed, captured through interpolation weights over a set of production-level breakpoints. The objective is to minimize the total cost over the year, discounted quarter by quarter, where cost combines direct production cost, inventory storage cost, and the cost of hiring and firing workers.

sets.name: Q
sets.doc: the ordered set of quarters in the planning year; the order is summer, fall, winter, spring, and it wraps around so that the quarter before summer is spring

sets.name: S
sets.doc: the set of work shifts that can be operated in a quarter

sets.name: L
sets.doc: the ordered set of production-level breakpoints, indexed by consecutive integers starting at 1, used to express the piecewise relationship between motors produced and labor required on a shift

params.name: mc
params.doc: material cost incurred per motor produced, in dollars per motor; a scalar

params.name: sr
params.doc: space rental rate charged per unit of inventory held, in dollars per motor; a scalar

params.name: hc
params.doc: cost of hiring one worker, in dollars per worker; a scalar

params.name: fc
params.doc: cost of firing one worker, in dollars per worker; a scalar

params.name: d
params.doc: motor demand to be met in each quarter, in motors; demand is zero in every quarter except spring

params.name: lc
params.doc: fixed leasing cost of the extra storage facility in each quarter, in dollars; charged only when the lease is taken

params.name: pr_labor
params.doc: workers required at production-level breakpoint l on a shift, in workers; together with pr_motor it defines the breakpoints of the piecewise motors-to-labor relationship

params.name: pr_motor
params.doc: motors produced at production-level breakpoint l on a shift, in motors; together with pr_labor it defines the breakpoints of the piecewise motors-to-labor relationship

params.name: sc_fixed
params.doc: fixed cost of operating a shift, in dollars, charged when the shift is used in a quarter

params.name: sc_labor
params.doc: cost per worker employed on a shift, in dollars per worker

params.name: delt
params.doc: discount factor applied to a quarter's cost, equal to one for the first quarter and shrinking geometrically for later quarters; a precomputed parameter

params.name: invmax
params.doc: an upper bound on inventory equal to the total annual demand, in motors; a precomputed scalar used to switch off the space-rental requirement when the extra storage facility is leased

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total discounted cost over the year, in thousands of dollars; this is the quantity the objective minimizes

vars.name: dpc
vars.doc: direct production cost in a quarter, in thousands of dollars, covering material, fixed shift, and shift labor costs

vars.name: isc
vars.doc: inventory storage cost in a quarter, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: wfc
vars.doc: workforce fluctuation cost in a quarter, in thousands of dollars, covering hiring and firing

vars.name: src
vars.doc: space rental cost in a quarter, in dollars, before being folded into the storage cost

vars.name: p
vars.doc: number of motors produced in a quarter

vars.name: ss
vars.doc: weight placed on production-level breakpoint l on a shift in a quarter; these weights interpolate between the breakpoints to express production and labor on that shift

vars.name: ssb
vars.doc: breakpoint selector indicating which production-level breakpoint is active on a shift in a quarter, used to keep the interpolation weights on adjacent breakpoints

vars.name: inv
vars.doc: motors held in inventory at the end of a quarter

vars.name: lease
vars.doc: whether the extra storage facility is leased for the year; one if leased, zero otherwise

vars.name: e
vars.doc: total number of workers employed in a quarter

vars.name: se
vars.doc: number of workers employed on a shift in a quarter

vars.name: shift
vars.doc: whether a shift is operated in a quarter; one if used, zero otherwise

vars.name: h
vars.doc: number of workers hired at the start of a quarter

vars.name: f
vars.doc: number of workers fired at the start of a quarter

description: The total cost for the year is the sum over the quarters of that quarter's combined production, storage, and workforce costs, with each quarter's contribution scaled down by its own discount factor so that costs further into the year count for less.

description: For each quarter the direct production cost accounts for the material cost of the motors produced that quarter together with, for every shift, the fixed cost of running that shift when it is operated and the per-worker cost of the workers staffed on it. Because the production cost is tracked in thousands of dollars while the underlying costs are in dollars, the total is rescaled to thousands.
expected_pyomo:
def ddpc_rule(model, q):
    return model.dpc[q] == (model.mc * model.p[q]
        + sum(model.sc_fixed[s] * model.shift[q, s] + model.sc_labor[s] * model.se[q, s]
              for s in model.S)) / 1000.0
model.ddpc = Constraint(model.Q, rule=ddpc_rule)

description: For each shift in each quarter the interpolation weights placed on the production-level breakpoints must together add up to whether that shift is operated, so the weights sum to one when the shift runs and to zero when it does not.

description: For each quarter the inventory carried at the end of the quarter equals the inventory brought in from the previous quarter plus the motors produced in the quarter minus the motors demanded in the quarter. For the very first quarter of the year there is no inventory carried in, so its ending inventory is simply production minus demand.

vars.doc: workforce fluctuation cost in a quarter, in thousands of dollars, covering hiring and firing

vars.name: src
vars.doc: space rental cost in a quarter, in dollars, before being folded into the storage cost

vars.name: p
vars.doc: number of motors produced in a quarter

vars.name: ss
vars.doc: weight placed on production-level breakpoint l on a shift in a quarter; these weights interpolate between the breakpoints to express production and labor on that shift

vars.name: ssb
vars.doc: breakpoint selector indicating which production-level breakpoint is active on a shift in a quarter, used to keep the interpolation weights on adjacent breakpoints

vars.name: inv
vars.doc: motors held in inventory at the end of a quarter

vars.name: lease
vars.doc: whether the extra storage facility is leased for the year; one if leased, zero otherwise

vars.name: e
vars.doc: total number of workers employed in a quarter

vars.name: se

vars.doc: whether a shift is operated in a quarter; one if used, zero otherwise

vars.name: f
vars.doc: number of workers fired at the start of a quarter

description: For each quarter the inventory storage cost combines the fixed leasing cost of the extra storage facility, charged only when the lease is taken for the year, with the space rental cost incurred that quarter. As with the other cost terms this is expressed in thousands of dollars, so the underlying dollar amounts are rescaled to thousands.

model_narrative: We plan motor production across the four quarters of a year. In each quarter we decide how many motors to produce, which shifts to operate and how many workers to staff on each, how many workers to hire or fire, and how much inventory to carry into the next quarter. We also decide once for the year whether to lease an extra storage facility. Production on each shift follows a piecewise relationship between motors made and workers needed, captured through interpolation weights over a set of production-level breakpoints. The objective is to minimize the total cost over the year, discounted quarter by quarter, where cost combines direct production cost, inventory storage cost, and the cost of hiring and firing workers.

sets.name: Q

sets.name: L
sets.doc: the ordered set of production-level breakpoints, indexed by consecutive integers starting at 1, used to express the piecewise relationship between motors produced and labor required on a shift

params.doc: an upper bound on inventory equal to the total annual demand, in motors; a precomputed scalar used to switch off the space-rental requirement when the extra storage facility is leased

description: For each quarter the space rental cost must be at least the rental rate applied to the inventory held that quarter, except that when the extra storage facility is leased for the year the chargeable inventory is reduced by the full inventory bound, which relaxes the requirement so that no space rental cost is forced. The rental cost is also kept nonnegative.

description: Employment evolves quarter to quarter as a steady cycle. For each quarter the workers employed equal the workers employed in the previous quarter plus those hired at the start of the quarter minus those fired, where the previous quarter wraps around so that the quarter before the first one is the last quarter of the year. This makes the workforce balance close on itself over the year.
expected_pyomo:
def eb2_rule(model, q):
    prev = model._prev_cyclic[q]
    return model.e[q] == model.e[prev] + model.h[q] - model.f[q]
model.eb2 = Constraint(model.Q, rule=eb2_rule)

description: The interpolation weights must stay on adjacent production-level breakpoints, consistent with the selected active breakpoint. For each production level, shift, and quarter, the weight on that level together with the weight on the level just below it cannot exceed the combined selection of that level and the two levels just below it. This forces the nonzero weights to sit on neighboring breakpoints around the chosen one.

params.name: lc
params.doc: fixed leasing cost of the extra storage facility in each quarter, in dollars; charged only when the lease is taken

params.name: pr_labor
params.doc: workers required at production-level breakpoint l on a shift, in workers; together with pr_motor it defines the breakpoints of the piecewise motors-to-labor relationship

params.name: pr_motor
params.doc: motors produced at production-level breakpoint l on a shift, in motors; together with pr_labor it defines the breakpoints of the piecewise motors-to-labor relationship

params.name: sc_fixed
params.doc: fixed cost of operating a shift, in dollars, charged when the shift is used in a quarter

params.name: sc_labor
params.doc: cost per worker employed on a shift, in dollars per worker

params.name: delt
params.doc: discount factor applied to a quarter's cost, equal to one for the first quarter and shrinking geometrically for later quarters; a precomputed parameter

params.name: invmax
params.doc: an upper bound on inventory equal to the total annual demand, in motors; a precomputed scalar used to switch off the space-rental requirement when the extra storage facility is leased

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total discounted cost over the year, in thousands of dollars; this is the quantity the objective minimizes

vars.name: dpc
vars.doc: direct production cost in a quarter, in thousands of dollars, covering material, fixed shift, and shift labor costs

vars.name: isc
vars.doc: inventory storage cost in a quarter, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: wfc
vars.doc: workforce fluctuation cost in a quarter, in thousands of dollars, covering hiring and firing

vars.name: src
vars.doc: space rental cost in a quarter, in dollars, before being folded into the storage cost

vars.name: p
vars.doc: number of motors produced in a quarter

vars.name: ss
vars.doc: weight placed on production-level breakpoint l on a shift in a quarter; these weights interpolate between the breakpoints to express production and labor on that shift

vars.name: ssb
vars.doc: breakpoint selector indicating which production-level breakpoint is active on a shift in a quarter, used to keep the interpolation weights on adjacent breakpoints

vars.name: inv
vars.doc: motors held in inventory at the end of a quarter

vars.name: lease
vars.doc: whether the extra storage facility is leased for the year; one if leased, zero otherwise

vars.name: e
vars.doc: total number of workers employed in a quarter

vars.name: se
vars.doc: number of workers employed on a shift in a quarter

vars.name: shift
vars.doc: whether a shift is operated in a quarter; one if used, zero otherwise

vars.name: h
vars.doc: number of workers hired at the start of a quarter

vars.name: f
vars.doc: number of workers fired at the start of a quarter

description: Generate the complete constraint set for this model.
